round_history.round0: Ran ONE measurement family (depth_reach). 14 branches cleared depth_lift>=2.0 & winner_deeper -> the iteration/path-depth subtype CONFIRMED. 39 failed -> rule_not_met.
round_history.round2: Kept depth_reach verbatim; ADDED a genuinely-different BUILT tool for the non-local face depth_reach is blind to: corpus_size_ratio (ctx_corpus_inflation). RAN it on s4 targets -> harfbuzz 0.924 / sqlite3 0.956 (both <1.0, entropy ~1.01): the corpus-inflation alternative was REFUTED, not confirmed. No branch rescued; rule kept as a falsifiable test that CAN fire on a truly-inflating target.
round_history.round3: This round. Asks whether ANY remaining BUILT tool (the pivots flagged but not yet tried: operand_enrichment, value_distance_reached, joint_necessity/token_count) discriminates a THIRD mechanism on the 39 residual. Conclusion below: none of them applies to a naive_ctx-vs-naive (havoc-only-vs-havoc-only) shape, so the residual is honestly decidable:false. Both priors KEPT verbatim (superset); NO new mechanism invented (G3).

hypotheses.label: naive_ctx's resolving corpus drives the blocker line DEEPER per seed (more loop/recursion/path re-entries, or actually takes the blocked side) than naive's blocking corpus
hypotheses.mechanism: CtxHook folds the caller chain into the edge-map index, so each loop re-entry / recursion depth level / distinct caller chain at the blocker registers as a fresh coverage bucket. This manufactures a depth gradient that retains seeds driving the blocker line to higher execution counts (and/or taking the blocked side) than naive's context-blind, saturated edge map. This is the per-seed-MEASURABLE face of the ctx advantage: the winner corpus reaches the blocker line deeper. Confirmed for 14 branches in round-0; this is the SAME rule, kept verbatim so the 14 stay validated and any further branch that genuinely clears the bar is caught.
hypotheses.prediction: naive_ctx's saved corpus drives the blocker line to a strictly higher per-seed execution count than naive's (depth_lift >= 2.0 AND winner_deeper == True). A value/byte-solving mechanism mis-bucketed into this naive_ctx>naive shape would reach the blocker line at the SAME depth and differ only in operand bytes -> depth_lift ~ 1.0 -> rule fails. A reversed depth (naive deeper, depth_lift < 1, winner_deeper == False, observed e.g. harfbuzz/5237 = 0.14) also fails. The rule is therefore falsifiable and discriminating.

hypotheses.label: naive_ctx hoards far more (context-redundant) saved-corpus entries than naive — a whole-campaign corpus-scale advantage independent of any single seed's terminal depth
hypotheses.mechanism: ctx_coverage is a NON-LOCAL feedback: its advantage is supposed to be that the (call-context, edge) map admits many more seeds — the same edge reached through a new caller chain counts as new coverage, so naive_ctx retains a much larger, more context-diverse corpus than naive's saturated bare-edge map. If that hoarding is real, the corpus-SCALE signature is visible whether or not any single terminal seed reaches the blocker deeper: a higher saved-corpus entry count for the naive_ctx arm. This is the second, corpus-scale face of the mechanism that depth_reach (per-seed terminal depth) is structurally blind to. It is arm-level (branch-independent): one (target, naive_ctx, naive) measurement labels every branch of that target.

params.corpus_count_ratio: median(#saved seeds naive_ctx) / median(#saved seeds naive) across trials

hypotheses.prediction: If ctx hoards a larger context-diverse corpus, the naive_ctx arm saves strictly MORE entries than naive (corpus_count_ratio >= 1.5). A 'no corpus-scale effect' alternative predicts ratio ~ 1.0, and a corpus-SHRINKING arm predicts ratio < 1. MEASURED on this shape's s4 targets: harfbuzz corpus_count_ratio = 0.924, sqlite3 = 0.956 (entropy ratios 1.016 / 1.011). So for harfbuzz and sqlite3 the corpus-inflation mechanism is REFUTED by the data. The rule is retained as a genuine falsifiable second-mechanism test (it CAN fire >=1.5 on a target where ctx truly inflates), but on the measured arms it fails — the correct, honest outcome.

rejected_pivots_this_round.why_inapplicable: It measures a cmplog-vs-naive byte enrichment at a fixed-offset VALUE gate. The RESOLVING arm here is naive_ctx (havoc-only + context feedback), NOT cmplog — there is no I2S operand-substitution contrast to read. The enrichment that the tool quantifies is a property of an I2S mutator the winning arm does not have. Applying it would manufacture a comparison the mechanism cannot produce (G2 violation: the resolve-pattern arm has no value-solver). Not proposed.

Several residual branches DO carry a literal operand (libpng color_type==0x03 at 3951/3966/3996/4085; libxml2 overlong-encoding <0x800 at 6887; harfbuzz codepoint switch arms 6130 0x11600 etc.), which is why this pivot was flagged. But the tool isolates a vp/vpc Hamming-gradient (CMP_MAP climbs toward the operand). The winning arm is naive_ctx, which has NEITHER value_profile NOR cmplog — no gradient mechanism exists to confirm. Worse, the cards state explicitly (e.g. libpng_3864 'no extra byte requirement beyond naive's') that the BYTES are not the blocker; RETENTION/scheduling is. So 'winner corpus lands byte-closer to the operand' is a stochastic byproduct of the SAME retention the two kept tools already test, not a distinct mechanism: a winner-closer AND a winner-not-closer result are both consistent with identical havoc + ctx retention. That fails G1 (not discriminating). Scope also bites: the color_type operand is width=1 (chance-alignment-prone per the tool's own scope_limits), and it reads on-disk seeds (s4-only). Not proposed.

Built for the I2S-x-VP joint-necessity ASSEMBLY-DEPTH subtype (vpc-resolves-only; vpc builds a larger structure than I2S-only reaches). This shape has a SINGLE resolving technique (naive_ctx) and no value_profile_cmplog arm — there is no vpc-vs-cmplog-vs-vp comparison to compute size_lift/tag_lift over. The structural-token count (size, tags) is a corpus-scale quantity already covered, in the relevant arm-pair, by corpus_size_ratio (which we ran and which refuted). Not proposed.

decidable_false_subgroup.label: non_local_ctx_no_seed_scale_signature
decidable_false_subgroup.criterion: branches that fail BOTH kept rules: depth_lift < 2.0 (per-seed terminal depth does not separate) AND corpus_count_ratio < 1.5 (the arm does not hoard a larger corpus). No additional BUILT tool discriminates them (operand_enrichment / value_distance_reached / joint_necessity are all inapplicable to a naive_ctx-vs-naive shape — see rejected_pivots_this_round).
decidable_false_subgroup.rationale: ctx_coverage's advantage on these branches operated at whole-campaign / scheduling scale (which seeds got energy, when) and left NO terminal corpus signature a built tool can read: the winner corpus neither reaches the blocker line deeper per seed nor is larger overall, and there is no value-solving (I2S/VP) mechanism on this arm whose operand-closure could be measured. This is exactly the spec's expected non-local / non-synthesizable case (guardrail G3). These branches are honest decidable:false / inconclusive, NOT a missing mechanism to be invented.
decidable_false_subgroup.note: decidable:false is recorded only where the kept tools actually ran and returned a measurement (n_scorable=2). It is NOT used because a target's corpus lives on the other server — every ctx target's naive_ctx/naive corpus exists on its owning server (s4: harfbuzz/sqlite3; sB: libpng/libxml2/lcms), so both kept tools are scorable on both servers; the owning server arbitrates its own targets.

notes.priors_kept: BOTH priors kept verbatim (superset / monotonic): iteration_path_depth_reach (depth_reach, rule depth_lift>=2.0 AND winner_deeper) and ctx_corpus_inflation (corpus_size_ratio, rule corpus_count_ratio>=1.5). The 14 round-0 confirmed branches re-validate under the unchanged hypothesis-1 rule.
notes.new_mechanisms_proposed: NONE. Round-3 evaluated the three remaining BUILT pivots (operand_enrichment, value_distance_reached, joint_necessity/token_count) and found all three inapplicable to a naive_ctx>naive (havoc-vs-havoc, context-feedback-only) shape — none introduces a discriminating contrast the resolving arm can actually produce. Inventing a value-precision mechanism here would violate G1 (not discriminating: the cards state the bytes are not the blocker) and G2 (the resolve-pattern arm has no value-solver). The honest outcome is convergence to decidable:false on the residual.
notes.decidable_false: The 39 unlabeled branches that fail both kept rules are honestly decidable:false (non_local_ctx_no_seed_scale_signature). This is the CORRECT G3 convergence for a non-local ctx family: no built tool discriminates the residual, so the loop is dry. Do NOT force labels.
notes.depth_near_miss_check: Round-2 re-ran depth_reach on a sample of the inconclusive harfbuzz branches: 5237 depth_lift=0.14 (winner_deeper=False, naive DEEPER), 5990 depth_lift=1.02 (flat — textbook no-corpus-effect), 5878 depth_lift=1.52 (thin near-miss clearing 1.5 but not 2.0). The signal degrades smoothly toward 1.0/<1.0. Threshold deliberately NOT lowered to 1.5 to harvest 5878: dropping below the 2.0 bar the 14 validated branches cleared would admit noise and contaminate the cohort (G3 honesty over coverage). Re-relaxing a refuted threshold is also forbidden by the round rules.
notes.G1_falsifiability: Both kept rules can come out the other way and rule out a concrete alternative. iteration_path_depth_reach: a value/byte mechanism predicts depth_lift~1.0 -> fails; observed reversals (5237=0.14) and flats (5990=1.02) DO fail it. ctx_corpus_inflation: a no-inflation alternative predicts ratio~1.0; the measured 0.924/0.956 fail it (a reported refutation, not a confirmation).
notes.G2_independence: Both measurements are independent of the resolve pattern that defined the shape (naive_ctx-resolves / naive-blocks, a coverage OUTCOME). depth_reach measures HOW DEEP each arm's corpus drives the blocker LINE; corpus_size_ratio measures HOW MANY entries each arm SAVED. Neither reads the resolve label.
notes.data_realities_respected: No seed_lineage / mutation_op events (the ctx effect is a feedback/corpus property, invisible in mutation_op; I2S is untagged; value_profile is a feedback not a mutator). Both kept tools read saved corpora / replay seeds, which exist on each target's OWNING server (s4: harfbuzz, sqlite3; sB: libpng, libxml2, lcms). The sB round-0 assignments confirm depth_reach already scored libpng/libxml2/lcms there (n_scorable=2), so corpus_size_ratio (same corpus source) is equally scorable on sB. decidable:false is an EVIDENCE-BACKED 'tools ran and said no', NOT an off-server skip. The rejected pivots were rejected on MECHANISM grounds (the resolving arm is havoc-only), valid for ALL branches on BOTH servers — not because their corpora are off-server.
